title: Adoniastrum vernale
intro:

Adonis vernalis, also known as Adonis or Adoniastrum vernale, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its showy flowers and toxic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Adonis vernalis include cardenolides, such as adonin and adonisonin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Adonis vernalis include extracts, tinctures, and teas, which are often used to treat fever and rheumatism.
